page: Microsoft Azure DC48as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCACCV5 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the DC48as_cc_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng tests and PassMark CPU scores. Its memory subsystem is highly efficient, achieving a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score, although uncached large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes remain in the average tier. Cryptographic capabilities are robust, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instructions, which is reflected in its strong PassMark encryption benchmark. Multi-threaded decompression and image processing tasks also perform in the top 25%. However, while small-model LLM inference is highly efficient, large-model text generation is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, which should be factored into architectural planning. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.

However, large-scale LLM text generation is a performance bottleneck. This server is highly suitable for multi-threaded enterprise applications, general-purpose web serving, and moderate database workloads.
